prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='DWT_svr', seasonality_champion='CWT_rf', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The true series shows consistent upward trends across all folds. DWT_svr best tracks trend patterns while CWT_rf captures seasonality most accurately. With rmse_spread_ratio of 0.89 and seasonality_corr_variance of 0.88, models disagree significantly on both trend and seasonal components. Early horizon specialists (THETA) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost), indicating horizon-specific performance patterns. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors while mitigating high disagreement risks.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "DWT_svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "CWT_rf",
  "overall_champion": "FT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "DWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_rf", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "FT_catboost", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "DWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["rf", "THETA", "ETS", "svr", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "FT_svr", "ARIMA"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.8856,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The true series shows consistent upward trends across all folds. DWT_svr best tracks trend patterns while CWT_rf captures seasonality most accurately. With rmse_spread_ratio of 0.89 and seasonality_corr_variance of 0.88, models disagree significantly on both trend and seasonal components. Early horizon specialists (THETA) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost), indicating horizon-specific performance patterns. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors while mitigating high disagreement risks."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k4"],
  "params_overrides": {"inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35": {"shrinkage": 0.35, "top_k": 5}, "topk_mean_per_horizon_k4": {"top_k": 4}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates high horizon heterogeneity requiring diverse selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=0.8856 suggests significant model disagreement, favoring dba_combination (recommended_method_hint) and shrinkage-weighted approaches. With only n_windows=3, shrinkage=0.35 prevents overfitting while topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 provides stability. Pattern analyst identified trend_champions (DWT_svr, DWT_catboost) and seasonality_champions (CWT_rf, FT_rf), so dba_combination leverages this diversity. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific patterns. inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage mitigates disagreement risks."
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["topk_mean_per_horizon_k10", "robust_median"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "n_windows=3 is low, so recommended_knobs suggests stronger regularization. RMSE stability flag shows unstable (rmse_std_over_rmse=0.295>0.3). Universe has topk_mean_per_horizon_k10 with RMSE=1973 vs current best 1998. robust_median achieves POCID=90.91 vs current 84.85. Adding these improves both accuracy and stability without removing existing diverse candidates.", "changes": ["added topk_mean_per_horizon_k10 for better RMSE (1973 vs 1998)", "added robust_median for POCID improvement (90.91 vs 84.85)"], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The true series shows consistent upward trends across all folds. DWT_svr best tracks trend patterns while CWT_rf captures seasonality most accurately. With rmse_spread_ratio of 0.89 and seasonality_corr_variance of 0.88, models disagree significantly on both trend and seasonal components. Early horizon specialists (THETA) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost), indicating horizon-specific performance patterns. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors while mitigating high disagreement risks.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates high horizon heterogeneity requiring diverse selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=0.8856 suggests significant model disagreement, favoring dba_combination (recommended_method_hint) and shrinkage-weighted approaches. With only n_windows=3, shrinkage=0.35 prevents overfitting while topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 provides stability. Pattern analyst identified trend_champions (DWT_svr, DWT_catboost) and seasonality_champions (CWT_rf, FT_rf), so dba_combination leverages this diversity. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific patterns. inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage mitigates disagreement risks.

statistician.rationale: n_windows=3 is low, so recommended_knobs suggests stronger regularization. RMSE stability flag shows unstable (rmse_std_over_rmse=0.295>0.3). Universe has topk_mean_per_horizon_k10 with RMSE=1973 vs current best 1998. robust_median achieves POCID=90.91 vs current 84.85. Adding these improves both accuracy and stability without removing existing diverse candidates.
changes: added topk_mean_per_horizon_k10 for better RMSE (1973 vs 1998)
added robust_median for POCID improvement (90.91 vs 84.85)
